intro: Celsia baetica, also known as Baetic Celsia, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its bright blue flowers and erect, branched stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various conditions, such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Celsia baetica include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Celsia baetica is used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to harness its medicinal benefits.
